rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GERALD A. DURKIN AND ELSIE J. DURKIN 2015 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions (Petition Filed In Consolidated Case 303954 On 5/3/21)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/23 to be heard with the new petition. Issues with the petition for instructions noted for the hearing on 3/3/21 still have not been addressed. The petitioner's address (not her attorney's) has never been set forth. No ex parte petition has been filed to dispense with notice to Charles Lucero, who is reportedly homeless and has not received notice of any of these hearings. The petition has never been supplemented to include a prayer, setting forth the instructions the petitioner seeks from the Court. Neither proof of service had a copy of the actual, mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 attached to it, showing the case and hearing information. Neither party has addressed under what power Elsie Durkin resigned as trustee and appointed Paul Lucero, when the trust named Jeri Rowland and Paul A. Lucero as successor trustees. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The account as filed does not balance. Total charges do not equal total credits as required by Probate Code section 1061(c). The total charges exceed the total credits by $438.91. This amount was reported as "unreconciled difference". The capital gain distributions ($8,303.90) were deducted from receipts. The net dividends used to purchase additional shares of Invesco and Nuveen mutual funds ($8,494.06 in total) were double-counted as receipts and also as additional property received as part of the charges. Even if those corrections were made, the account would not balance in that the total charges would still exceed the total credits by $248.75 (which could be some expenses reportable as disbursements). The Cour is to determine whether to allow and settle the unbalanced account as filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTH JOHNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/21/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition caption includes settlement of a final account (in addition to a final report), but no accounting schedules were attached. If the petitioner is requesting approval of a final report on waiver of account, the petition caption must be amended and renoticed. 4) The account statement shows the petitioner's title as trustee of the devisee trust. Has the petitioner distributed all the assets to the devisee trust without prior court approval? 5) Each asset on hand must be specifically described. CRC 7.651. A specific amount of cash on hand as of a date certain must be alleged. The name and number of shares of each security as of a date certain must be alleged. If the number of shares is different from the number listed on the inventory and appraisal, the difference must be explained, even if the account is waived. CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3). 6) WBD shares are not listed on the inventory and appraisal. How did the petitioner acquire those shares? 7) A detailed schedule of losses must be filed in support of the statutory fee base. CRC 7.550(b)(6). 8) The cash on hand appears insufficient to pay the statutory fees and reserve. The securities must be partially liquidated to increase the estate cash on hand.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALZINA MARIE RAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Distribute Share Of Estate Of Heir Marcus Mcclure To Special Needs Trust For His Benefit
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/2023, as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 08/21/2023 hearing) remain unaddresed: If there is no conservatorship case, a petition to establish a special needs trust must be filed in a new trust case which must be opened under the name of the proposed trust. The following issues must also be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. A copy of the proposed trust must be attached to the order. LRSF 14.48(B). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Please provide a specific description and value of each asset distributable to Marcus McClure from this estate, together with annual income from each asset, for the purpose of setting the trustee's initial bond. 4) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for Marcus McClure. Does he qualify as a person who lacks legal capacity to make decisions within the meaning of Probate Code section 1003(a)(2)? 5) A budget of anticipated expenditures to be paid out of the proposed trust must be provided. LRSF 14.49(B). 6) Please identify all public benefits received by Marcus McClure. 7) Please identify issue of Marcus McClure. If he has no issue, please identify his heirs. 8) Does the petitioner, if appointed as trustee, intend to carry on the day-to-day activity of the trust in the county where the petitioner resides? If so, should the Court transfer jurisdiction to the county of the petitioner's residence per section 3604(a)(1)?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GREGSON 1996 TRUST, DATED JUNE 28, 1996

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAVID ALLAN HYMAN TRUST DATED 9/6/22
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required for failure to address issues raised at the hearing on 5/8/23. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar for failure to address defects. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
